name: Spinach

pros.title: elite micronutrient density
pros.explanation: This is exactly the kind of nutrient-packed vegetable the category is supposed to reward.

pros.title: very light for the volume
pros.explanation: A practical plus for a maintenance-and-repair food.

pros.title: easy to use in lots of meals
pros.explanation: Works raw, cooked, blended, and as a side or add-in.

cons.title: contains anti-nutrients
cons.explanation: Oxalates can reduce mineral usefulness in some contexts.

cons.title: volume shrinks a lot when cooked
cons.explanation: Can make portions feel smaller than expected.

cons.title: not useful as a fuel food
cons.explanation: That is fine for vegetables, but it does limit some use cases.

sourceNotes: Values are approximate representative placeholders for ruleset tuning, not a clinical nutrient database.
